action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline ResNet50+LSTM (global pool, no attention) after 3 epochs has train loss ~2.6 but val exprate=0.0; debug shows mostly invalid preds like '\sqrt { \mbox \mbox }', '2 = 1', repetitive \mbox (vocab token). Analysis: Pooled vector loses 2D spatial structure essential for math layout (fractions, scripts, sums); simple LSTM can't align tokens to image regions. Decision: Major arch upgrade to ResNet feature maps (conv output flattened to seq of 16 vectors) + LSTM decoder with additive Bahdanau-style attention + mean-pool init for h/c; update Encoder/Decoder/MathExprRecognizer, add Attention nn.Module; set encoded_dim=512, hidden=256; update train.py to 5 epochs + explicit init + full code; batch predict.py for efficiency; rewrite debug.py for new model + sample preds. Train+debug then submit_eval on best.pt to get new baseline score and iteration timing.
